title: Moonshine River

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening, presumably Homer encountering some mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but not original at this point in the series

jokes.text: which, like all our town showcase events, is not only poorly planned, it's horribly executed.
jokes.setup: Kent reporting on Springfield Grand Prix as a showcase event
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting it as poorly planned AND horribly executed
jokes.explanation: Good structure with escalation from 'poorly planned' to 'horribly executed', fits Kent's cynical news voice

jokes.text: The Making of the Empire State Building, Part One: The Basement.
jokes.setup: Documentary title appears on screen
jokes.punchline: Mundane focus on just the basement of an iconic building
jokes.explanation: Clever anticlimax - promises grand documentary about iconic building, delivers basement focus

jokes.text: Homer, we can buy Scotch tape tomorrow. There's the gap!
jokes.setup: Family apparently trying to cross a dangerous gap at the race
jokes.punchline: Marge prioritizes buying tape over immediate safety
jokes.explanation: Marge's practical priorities clashing with immediate danger is classic character work

jokes.text: Apparently, today is also the final stage of the Tour De Springfield bike race.
jokes.setup: Confusion at the race event
jokes.punchline: Casual discovery of major oversight in event planning
jokes.explanation: Springfield's incompetence theme - two major events scheduled simultaneously

jokes.text: Truthfully, today, there were no losers and no winners since both finish lines were tangled up in this unholy mess.
jokes.setup: Expected wrap-up of who won the races
jokes.punchline: Nobody won because the finish lines are physically tangled
jokes.explanation: Visual absurdity with good escalation - from poor planning to literal tangled mess

jokes.text: Well, uh, let's, uh, all start tapping our toes to the sounds of Scab Calloway and his non-union band.
jokes.setup: Need for entertainment to distract from disaster
jokes.punchline: 'Scab Calloway' - pun on Cab Calloway with union-busting reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining jazz legend with labor issues - classic Simpsons cultural reference humor

jokes.text: Meaningless friend thing. True love super date!
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if this is a real date
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's overeager contradiction of his own downplaying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - tries to play it cool then immediately reveals his desperation

jokes.text: it'll look something like this [baby visual]
jokes.setup: Bart talking about Lisa and Milhouse having a baby
jokes.punchline: Presumably shows some horrific hybrid visualization
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart antagonism, likely with disturbing visual - standard sibling torment

jokes.text: Don't listen to Uncle Bart. Lisa and I will raise you in a house of love.
jokes.setup: Bart joking about Lisa and Milhouse having a baby
jokes.punchline: Milhouse immediately planning their future family life
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's delusional romantic projections - uncomfortable but perfectly in character

jokes.text: Geek leak!
jokes.setup: Bart sees Milhouse dancing awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'gas leak' but with 'geek'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun - efficient but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: Dance with me, please. I need to zing my sister.
jokes.setup: Bart asking someone to dance
jokes.punchline: His motive is purely to antagonize Lisa
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - using honesty about his terrible motives as a pickup line

jokes.text: Okay, but you have to do your homework. Just kidding. I've given up on that.
jokes.setup: Bart agreeing to dance with someone
jokes.punchline: Fake condition followed by admission he's given up on homework entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - brief attempt at responsibility immediately abandoned with casual honesty

jokes.text: Just move your girdle, Yertle.
jokes.setup: Bart telling someone to get out of the way
jokes.punchline: Dr. Seuss reference ('Yertle the Turtle') + old-fashioned insult ('girdle')
jokes.explanation: Creative insult combining children's literature with dated fashion reference

jokes.text: Hey, Lise, your dance partner just made the cover Dorks Illustrated magazine. I know that's a zing, but still... the cover!
jokes.setup: Bart insulting Lisa's date
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging it's an insult but being impressed by magazine cover status anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - insult followed by self-aware contradiction showing mixed feelings about achievement

jokes.text: Please let there be a 'but' coming, please! But... Landed it!
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Milhouse as 'not perfect'
jokes.punchline: Milhouse desperately hoping for qualification, celebrating when it comes
jokes.explanation: Great meta-awareness of speech patterns with Milhouse's pathetic hopefulness perfectly executed

jokes.text: Hey, plenty of babes have docked in Porta de Barto.
jokes.setup: Bart defending his romantic history
jokes.punchline: Port/harbor metaphor with 'Barto' name pun
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor with decent wordplay, though setup is a bit forced

jokes.text: But as soon as they get to know the real you, they departo de Barto
jokes.setup: Lisa countering Bart's romantic boasting
jokes.punchline: Extending his pun to make the opposite point
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that tops the original joke with perfect wordplay and sibling dynamic

jokes.text: Yeah, for about a week. But as soon as they get to know the real you, they departo de Barto.
jokes.setup: Lisa countering Bart's boast about his dating success
jokes.punchline: Continuing his port metaphor with 'departo de Barto' - they leave port
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and extension of Bart's metaphor, perfect sibling comeback with linguistic creativity

jokes.text: If the late, great Nora Ephron taught us anything, it's... Oh! What's my other inflatable doll doing here?
jokes.setup: Homer starting to give romantic advice referencing famous screenwriter
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by discovering his inflatable doll at the dance
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection from highbrow to lowbrow, excellent interruption structure, very Homer

jokes.text: Three days. One recess. Half a field trip.
jokes.setup: Bart reviewing how long his relationships lasted
jokes.punchline: Progressively shorter durations ending with partial field trip
jokes.explanation: Perfect rule-of-three escalation with excellent economy - 'half a field trip' is brilliantly specific and pathetic

jokes.text: Boy, if anyone needs me, I'll be taking a popcorn bath. It's a thing I read about in a Men's Health magazine in a dream.
jokes.setup: Bart announcing his depression ritual
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous source: magazine article from a dream
jokes.explanation: Wonderful absurdist escalation - starts with weird self-care, ends with dream magazine as source

jokes.text: Look in the mirror, unplanned miracle.
jokes.setup: Bart asking how Homer got Marge to stay
jokes.punchline: Homer calling Bart an 'unplanned miracle' - backhanded compliment about pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Dark but affectionate Homer parenting - implies Bart was accident but frames positively

jokes.text: All right, who took the microwave?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he keeps things interesting
jokes.punchline: Marge discovering missing appliance proves his point
jokes.explanation: Good timing - perfectly proves Homer's point about keeping things interesting through chaos

jokes.text: Hey, it's me again. He's there, isn't he? Well, I hope this doesn't sound weird, but I'm inside your closet.
jokes.setup: Homer demonstrating drunk dialing to express unfiltered feelings
jokes.punchline: Escalates from jealous call to revealing he's stalking/breaking and entering
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from pathetic to criminal behavior, shows Homer's terrible romantic advice

jokes.text: Eat my shorts! Drop dead. Eat fist, jerk. You've got a lot of nerve showing up here... and not giving me a kiss.
jokes.setup: Series of hostile rejections from Bart's ex-girlfriends
jokes.punchline: Final girl subverts pattern by being upset he didn't kiss her
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - establishes pattern of hostility then subverts with romantic expectation

jokes.text: Yeah, she liked to drink milk as a kid instead of white paint.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting that Mary is cute for Cletus's daughter
jokes.punchline: Cletus's measure of intelligence/normalcy is not drinking paint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - reveals horrifying baseline for hillbilly family while maintaining Cletus's proud tone

jokes.text: Prove it. A, B, C... All right. All right. You convinced me, Einstein.
jokes.setup: Cletus demanding Bart prove he's not one of his kids
jokes.punchline: Alphabet knowledge as proof of not being his child, calling Bart 'Einstein'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus logic - basic literacy as genius-level achievement in his family

jokes.text: She ran away after we engaged her to Old Man Wellbottom. And I was gonna be a good husband, too! If'n I ever get out of this well.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Mary ran away - forced engagement
jokes.punchline: Fiancé is literally stuck in a well, still optimistic about marriage prospects
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark absurdism - arranged marriage to man trapped in well, his continued optimism

jokes.text: New York? That's where Sesame Street is. Do you ever wonder what happens to Oscar on garbage day?
jokes.setup: Cletus identifying New York City
jokes.punchline: His main association is Sesame Street, with oddly thoughtful question about Oscar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Cletus's childlike NYC reference combined with unexpectedly insightful question

jokes.text: Daddy just covers things with hay and says they're done.
jokes.setup: Warning about unsafe floor
jokes.punchline: Cletus's universal 'fix' using hay
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that's both funny and believable for Cletus

jokes.text: 'Cause the floor ain't finished. Daddy just covers things with hay and says they're done.
jokes.setup: Mary warning about safety upstairs
jokes.punchline: Revealing Cletus's construction 'method' - hay over unfinished work
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Cletus's lazy construction methods revealed through Mary's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: And $500 for Yankee tickets, which turn out to be bogus. Well, don't buy 'em. But they're playing the Purple Sox.
jokes.setup: Homer listing NYC expenses including fake Yankee tickets
jokes.punchline: Homer insisting on buying despite warning because of fake team 'Purple Sox'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - knows tickets are fake but wants them anyway for nonsensical reason (fake team)

jokes.text: Now, that your two least favorite buildings Old Penn Station and Shea Stadium. Lousy, outdated relics!
jokes.setup: Homer saying he loves New York
jokes.punchline: His favorite thing is that iconic buildings were demolished - calls them relics
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Homer appreciates NYC for destroying its own landmarks, shows his philistine nature

jokes.text: Keep your meat hooks off my sister Marge or my goons will do a soft show on your solar plexus.
jokes.setup: Homer remembering his tough NYC persona
jokes.punchline: 'Soft show' instead of 'shiv' or violent threat - mixing tough talk with gentle language
jokes.explanation: Good parody of tough guy speak with Homer's linguistic confusion creating accidental gentleness

jokes.text: Homer, that sounds like The Sweet Smell of Success. It didn't feel like success.
jokes.setup: Marge identifying Homer's dialogue as from a famous film
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation - it didn't feel successful to him
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Marge's cultural knowledge vs Homer's literal mindedness

jokes.text: I didn't say I swapped our house.
jokes.setup: Concern about house-swappers wrecking their home
jokes.punchline: Homer swapped someone else's house, not theirs
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection - reveals Homer's solution involves screwing over someone else

jokes.text: Lord, you're testing me, aren't you? Yeah, keep talking to your friend up there. We'll be doing it on your bed. New York style.
jokes.setup: Ned realizing he's been stuck with house guests
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude threat about defiling Ned's bed 'New York style'
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Ned's religious resignation vs Homer's crude territorial marking

jokes.text: Fievel was a mouse in a movie. Yes, and now look at us. Only in America.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming ancestry to Fievel the immigrant mouse
jokes.punchline: Homer doubling down on fictional mouse heritage as American success story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - corrected about fictional mouse but treats it as real family history

jokes.text: 50,000 square feet, huh? All Khlav Kalash! Used to be bookstore. Books.
jokes.setup: Street vendor discussing his large space
jokes.punchline: Massive bookstore replaced entirely by mysterious ethnic food, dismissive 'Books'
jokes.explanation: Good cultural commentary on gentrification/commercialization, vendor's dismissive tone about books

jokes.text: You know you're missing your clothes? I thought this was just a dream.
jokes.setup: Someone apparently naked in public
jokes.punchline: Person thinks being naked in NYC is a dream
jokes.explanation: Standard 'naked in public' dream reference - familiar but executed adequately

jokes.text: Anything you leave in a cab you'll never see again.
jokes.setup: Family getting out of cab
jokes.punchline: Driver's honest warning about lost items
jokes.explanation: Basic NYC stereotype - honest but unhelpful cab driver warning, minimal craft

jokes.text: Which is that love doesn't exist, except briefly between a man and a woman before marriage. After that, it's just hanging out with someone who kind of hates you but you can't get it together to leave.
jokes.setup: Homer offering life lesson about love to Bart
jokes.punchline: Cynical definition of marriage as mutual trapped resentment
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest marital humor - probably too close to truth for many viewers

jokes.text: I'm a featured player on Saturday Night Live. Aw, I'm lying. My whole life here is a bunch of lies. I'm just a writer with a performing option.
jokes.setup: Mary revealing her impressive NYC job
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflating it to lesser writing position, then condemning herself for lying
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and honesty about NYC struggling artist life - captures real industry dynamics

jokes.text: Okay, second balcony, partially obstructed view, no bathroom privileges and you must stipulate that, in emergencies, you can change a spotlight shell.
jokes.setup: Trying to get affordable Broadway tickets
jokes.punchline: Terrible seats with emergency work requirements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of terrible theater conditions, emergency labor requirement is nice absurd touch

jokes.text: Plus, I thought I might get to see an air conditioner fall on someone's head. But I guess that never really... D'oh! Ow! What was that?! Is that your father?
jokes.setup: Bart hoping to see NYC cliché of falling air conditioner
jokes.punchline: Immediately happens to Homer as Bart speaks
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and structure - perfectly executed expectation followed by immediate fulfillment

jokes.text: Plus, I thought I might get to see an air conditioner fall on someone's head. But I guess that never really... D'oh! Ow! What was that?!
jokes.setup: Bart expressing NYC expectation about falling air conditioner
jokes.punchline: Immediate fulfillment with Homer as victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and immediate payoff structure with classic Simpsons timing

jokes.text: We're safe because you have to climb steps to get up here. Can't move? No.
jokes.setup: Mary explaining they're safe from Homer on elevated walkway
jokes.punchline: Homer is immobilized, presumably from air conditioner injury
jokes.explanation: Good callback to air conditioner incident, efficient visual comedy payoff

jokes.text: Giuliani!
jokes.setup: Discussion of no crime in New York
jokes.punchline: Homer's one-word tribute to former mayor as crime-fighter
jokes.explanation: Simple reference humor - economical but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: No, Lisa! If you click that turnstile, it'll blow our budget! Aah! Whaah!
jokes.setup: Family trying to find free culture in NYC
jokes.punchline: Even subway entrance threatens their tiny budget, Lisa's distress
jokes.explanation: Good poverty humor - even basic transit is luxury they can't afford, Lisa's cultural desperation

jokes.text: Well, without them around, you and I can walk through the city holding hands, just like the menfolk do.
jokes.setup: Mary noting they're alone in the city
jokes.punchline: Her innocent country view of NYC men holding hands
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Mary's rural innocence misinterpreting NYC gay culture as general male behavior

jokes.text: Rolling on my skateboard, pimping like a drug lord...
jokes.setup: Mary singing country song about Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriate rap insertion about criminal lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - trying to sound cool in sweet romantic context, misses the tone completely

jokes.text: Rolling on my skateboard, pimping like a drug lord... [rap interruption]
jokes.setup: Bart doing rap despite being told not to
jokes.punchline: Age-inappropriate lyrics about drug dealing
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment showing his inappropriate understanding of rap culture

jokes.text: That's 'cause the donkey busted her jaw. She'll be fine. Your mother too.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Mary's mother can't express missing her
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of donkey violence, unclear if mother IS the donkey
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark absurdism - ambiguous relationship between mother and donkey, Cletus's casual delivery

jokes.text: That's not iambic pentameter!
jokes.setup: Director making announcement at Shakespeare in the Park
jokes.punchline: Audience member critiquing the announcement's meter
jokes.explanation: Good NYC intellectual culture satire - audience expects even announcements to be in proper meter

jokes.text: You guys suck at acting and you ate all the potato salad. No, you suck at acting and it's first come, first serve! I made that potato salad! Well, uh, idiot, it says Zaybar's on the package! He works at Zaybar's.
jokes.setup: Actor families feuding and quitting Shakespeare production
jokes.punchline: Feud over potato salad, revelation that homemade claim was false but defender works at deli
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity satire with absurd escalation - from acting to catering disputes to employment revelation

jokes.text: Faster, man! More annoying!
jokes.setup: Passing drum circle in park
jokes.punchline: Leader encouraging maximum irritation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy capturing everyone's feelings about drum circles

jokes.text: Well, my only professional job was playing a nefarious hot dog in an ad for Taco Bueno. You fresh-made tacos will never defeat pre-boiled hot dogs!
jokes.setup: Struggling actor begging for role in amateur Shakespeare
jokes.punchline: His only credit is playing evil hot dog in taco commercial with ridiculous dialogue
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist specificity - evil hot dog villain with food war dialogue perfectly captures struggling actor desperation

jokes.text: Please, I owe Yale Drama School $200,000. They keep calling my mother.
jokes.setup: Actor continuing to plead for amateur role
jokes.punchline: Massive debt from prestigious school, creditors calling his mom
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on arts education economics - prestigious training leads to debt and hot dog commercials

jokes.text: Those the people who took over the theatre? Pepper spray and power wash-- the New York hug and kiss.
jokes.setup: Police arriving at amateur Shakespeare takeover
jokes.punchline: Describing violent crowd control as NYC affection
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - reframing police violence as NYC hospitality, economical and quotable

jokes.text: Here you go, little fan. Killer storm on the way. Um, I gonna go try to sell this. I'll buy it back.
jokes.setup: Bart getting Al Roker's autograph
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately planning to sell it, Roker offering to buy it back
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo humor - Bart's immediate mercenary instinct, Roker's good-natured response

jokes.text: there'll be other Mary Spucklers, including my sisters Mary Zeke and Mary Not Quite Right.
jokes.setup: Mary trying to comfort Bart about their separation
jokes.punchline: Revealing her sisters' names - one masculine, one literally indicating mental issues
jokes.explanation: Excellent hillbilly naming conventions - 'Mary Zeke' and 'Mary Not Quite Right' perfectly capture family's approach

jokes.text: there'll be other Mary Spucklers, including my sisters Mary Zeke and Mary Not Quite Right
jokes.setup: Mary comforting Bart about their separation
jokes.punchline: Family naming convention with concerning final name
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail with escalating absurdity in naming pattern

jokes.text: Bart, if any girl tries to fix you, let 'em, because you got a couple of big problems. But mostly, you're great!
jokes.setup: Mary giving Bart romantic advice
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment acknowledging his problems but affirming his worth
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Mary's honest but loving assessment perfectly captures young romance dynamic

jokes.text: you got a couple of big problems. But mostly, you're great!
jokes.setup: Mary giving Bart honest assessment
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment structure
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor with honest but encouraging tone

jokes.text: I can't because Mary's the girl that proves that girls can like me. And she wouldn't like me anymore if I gave her up. So I won't, even if you torture me like you do the English language.
jokes.setup: Cletus demanding Bart reveal Mary's location
jokes.punchline: Bart's principled refusal ending with insult about Cletus's grammar
jokes.explanation: Great character growth moment - Bart showing integrity with perfect zinger about Cletus's speech

jokes.text: Well, if that ain't aren't the isn't.
jokes.setup: Cletus trying to express surprise at Bart's response
jokes.punchline: Hilariously mangled attempt at proper grammar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Cletus trying to correct his grammar and failing spectacularly, perfect economy

jokes.text: Uh, one on-getter for the clickity clack, please.
jokes.setup: Cletus buying train ticket
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemisms for train travel
jokes.explanation: Excellent Cletus vocabulary with perfect onomatopoeia for trains

jokes.text: No kidding. I saw your reviews. Hey! I specifically said, no critics!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Romeo and Juliet ending badly
jokes.punchline: Homer mentions reading reviews, director's outrage at critics attending
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor about amateur theater's relationship with criticism, adequately executed

tmdb_overview: Bart will be visited by many of his past girlfriends.
